name: Multi Surface Single Engine
summary: One core engine — a shell script, AppleScript, API, or binary — wrapped by multiple UX surfaces that each reach different users: a web dashboard, a CLI…
description: One core engine — a shell script, AppleScript, API, or binary — wrapped by multiple UX surfaces that each reach different users: a web dashboard, a CLI command, a Shortcut, a menu-bar widget, a background agent, an SSH-safe headless mode. Each surface is a thin adapter. The engine never changes to accommodate a surface. Triggers on "multiple ways to run this", "add a CLI for this tool", "same tool as a Shortcut and a web UI", "don't duplicate the logic", "headless mode for CI", "menu bar and dashboard", "single source of truth for the cleanup logic".
author: marvelousempire
goal: One engine powers every surface — the logic is never duplicated across CLI, web, Shortcut, or launchd.
